name: Tyquan Thornton
headline: Field-stretching boom-bust dart joins loaded Chiefs
body:
Thornton finally flashed in 2025, posting career bests of 438 yards and three scores on just 19 catches, ranking WR65. The how is extreme: a 26.89 air-yards-per-target and 52.37 air yards per reception are pure vertical usage, fueling a gaudy +0.709 EPA per play and 1.89 yards per route run on only a 6.7 percent target share. Seven catches of 20-plus yards and a 61-yard long underline the boom, but a 51.4 catch rate, three drops, and a 39 percent snap share on 12 games expose the bust. It dwarfs his 138 combined yards across 2023-2024.

The move to Kansas City is a fit on paper but a usage minefield. He profiles as the designated deep threat behind Rashee Rice, Xavier Worthy, and Travis Kelce, meaning a thin target diet tethered to Patrick Mahomes hitting low-probability shots. That means a handful of spike weeks buried among zeros. For redraft, Thornton is a late-round dart and best-ball stash, not a lineup option you can trust weekly.
